title: androsace ochotensis var. arctica
intro: Androsace ochotensis var. arctica, also known as Arctic Rock Jasmine, is a perennial plant species in the Primulaceae family that is native to the Arctic regions of Russia and Alaska. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as its ability to improve cardiovascular health and boost the immune system. Androsace ochotensis var. arctica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive disorders.
